differential_diagnoses: Alzheimer's disease with comorbid cerebral amyloid angiopathy
Primary angiitis of the central nervous system (PACNS)
next_steps: Send CSF for autoimmune encephalitis panel including LGI1, CASPR2, and Hu/ANNA-1 antibodies
Obtain whole-body PET-CT scan to screen for occult malignancy
Initiate empiric high-dose intravenous glucocorticoids for possible CAA-ri or inflammatory etiology
Continue antiepileptic therapy with lacosamide and monitor with continuous EEG
Obtain MRI brain with follow-up susceptibility-weighted imaging to monitor hemosiderin staining and edema

differential_diagnoses: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system (PACNS)
Alzheimer's disease with comorbid cerebral amyloid angiopathy
next_steps: Perform CSF cytology and flow cytometry for malignant cells
Calculate CSF IgG index to interpret the 16 oligoclonal bands
Obtain serum and CSF autoimmune encephalitis panels including anti-Hu, anti-LGI1, and anti-CASPR2
Proceed with PET-CT for systemic malignancy screening
Initiate trial of intravenous corticosteroids (e.g., methylprednisolone) for suspected CAA-ri
Maintain antiseizure therapy with lacosamide and monitor with continuous EEG
Obtain brain MRI with and without gadolinium, including repeat susceptibility-weighted imaging to monitor for progression of hemosiderin staining and edema
